original_image_tags: Scanned newspaper clipping from the Times-News, Twin Falls, Idaho. The clipping is yellowed and shows signs of age. The main section is titled "Twin Falls News in Brief." It is surrounded by columns of other news items, including "Four Parleys Set on Cassia School Plans," "Funeral Held for Infant at Wendell," "Jenkins May Try Run Tests Friday," and obituaries for William Munger and Harry Piper. There is a small handwritten note in the right margin near the top of the "Four Parleys" article that reads "26".

elements.description: A tattered, irregularly torn newspaper clipping on a dark background. The masthead reads 'TIMES-NEWS, TWIN FALLS, IDaho'. It features several columns of text with multiple article headlines including 'Twin Falls News in Brief', 'Four Parleys Set on Cassia School Plant', 'Funeral Held for Infant at Wendell', 'Harry Piper, 71, Claimed by Death', 'William Munger, 38, Passes Here', and 'Jenkins May Try Run Tests Friday'. The clipping is heavily torn on the left and right edges, obscuring parts of some articles. There are no photographs, diagrams, or other graphical elements on the clipping itself, only typed text arranged in columns.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the newspaper clipping's masthead, column structure, and headlines, but Description A mentions a handwritten note '26' that Description B does not, which is a detail discrepancy without contradiction.
